page: Microsoft Azure E2as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EASv5 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The AMD EPYC processor delivers strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, due to the low core count of 2 vCPUs, multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench is weak, falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor results, with read-only large block bandwidth at 30.8 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 22.14 GB/sec. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, while database operations and Redis SET operations demonstrate poor throughput, reflecting the resource constraints of a smaller instance size.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E2as_v5 offers a low-entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. The high memory density per vCPU makes it a viable option for small-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-bound applications. However, the lack of bundled local storage and poor multi-core scaling represent significant tradeoffs. This server is best suited for lightweight enterprise applications, development environments, and small-scale memory-optimized workloads such as basic web serving or low-concurrency text processing.
description: Microsoft Azure E2as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EASv5 family, featuring an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 3.525 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. While stress-ng benchmarks highlight strong single-core CPU performance, the instance exhibits weak multi-core scaling and poor memory bandwidth under heavy loads. No local storage is bundled, though the instance is Premium Storage capable. This server represents a cost-efficient entry point for memory-bound workloads that do not demand high parallel processing power. It is best suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, lightweight web serving, and development environments requiring a higher memory footprint relative to compute capacity.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and a high memory-to-core ratio for lightweight, memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance but limited multi-core scaling
No bundled local storage, with support for Premium Storage
Best suited for small-scale databases, caching, and development environments
